item.text: ​Video Art Visions: Romain Gavras & Surkin - Baikonur Imagining futures glimpsed from the margins, shaped less by collapse than by distortion, French filmmaker Romain Gavras and musician-producer Surkin build a multimedia world around peripheral dystopias for GENER8ION: Visions of 2034 – a major exhibition at London’s 180 Studios . The first of ten audiovisual works, observing familiar yet skewed environments, their experimental film Baikonur documents the world’s oldest space launch facility, the Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan – a shadow of the Soviet era that remains under Russian control. Reflecting on how culture is consumed, processed, and forgotten, the video piece sits within a wider concept examining mutations between the reflexes of pleasure and the machinery of control – in response to cultural theorist Jean Baudrillard’s concept of simulation. Meditating on the spaceport’s unchanged infrastructure, and the moment a rocket launches into space, Gavras and Surkin consider the philosophy of freedom from a site that occupies a strange space in history – existing as a vision of what survives, and what doesn’t in an uncertain future. What first brought you to Kazakhstan? Romain Gavras: It’s the second time I went. I love the "Stans" – the layers of history, ancient and recent, in that part of the world are worn on the sleeves How did you land on the idea for the film? I was originally scouting for a secret project. We went with my line producer Guillaume Le François and my DOP André Chemetoff and when we got there we couldn’t stop shooting. What drew your attention to the Baikonur Cosmodrome? I’ve always been fascinated by it. It’s a Russian built enclave in the middle of the Kazakh desert, only made to support the Cosmodrome – like an ex-Soviet Truman Show for space exploration. At the time, it was the only place on Earth where they launched humans into space, so even our astronauts had to use the Russian infrastructures of Roscosmos to get up to space. It was also the only place in the world where you were allowed to film a rocket launched from only a kilometer away – Cape Canaveral, for example, is 8km. How did you go about creating a sense of dystopia? I didn’t really have to, it’s there. Built in the ‘50s , nothing has really moved, there is an amazing sense of purpose in the architecture of it, a sense that the future was going to be glorious – then time froze everything in that period. When I shot it, the only humans going to space were launched from there and every technological aspect is from the ‘70s – the bus the astronauts arrive at the rocket in, the train the rocket travels in. It’s fascinating. How did you guide the visual approach? It was really meant to be documentation for my own research, so it is very removed from a strong intention, very matter of fact and photo-like. How has your creative relationship with Surkin evolved? We’ve known each other since forever, and always liked to work together and talk about ideas about music and images. Can you talk us through some of the film’s themes, and how this relates to the exhibition as a whole? We open the exhibition with it. I feel it gives context about how history moves and the sentiment of the exhibit as a whole, which is constructed as a retrospective about a time in the future. Within this body of work, what message did you want the film to communicate? I’m not keen on straightforward messages, but I like the idea of creating a feeling, and the feelings I have about the future are dual. One take is that no matter what happens, there is still going to be poetry and beauty in the world, and kids will be kids doing silly things no matter the environment. My other take is that we all know that we’re dancing on the edge of a volcano. GENER8ION: Visions of 2034 will be on view at 180 Studios in London until 30 August 2026.
item.title: ​Video Art Visions: Romain Gavras & Surkin - Baikonur

item.text: 「フジロック フェスティバル’26（FUJI ROCK FESTIVAL'26）」の開幕まで、あと2日。登場するアーティストのファッションにも毎年注目が集まりますが、昨年のフジロックで最も服装が話題になったミュージシャンの1人として挙げられるのは、「カトパコ」こと「カトリエル&パコ・アモロソ（CA7RIEL & Paco Amoroso）」ではないでしょうか。 彼らがステージで着用したのは、「アンリアレイジ（ANREALAGE）」が2025年春夏コレクションで発表した「空調服」*とのコラボレーションアイテムです。 *「空調服」は株式会社空調服のファン付きウェア、その附属品、及びこれらを示すブランド これまで空調ウェアが着用されていたのは、建設業や運送業などのタフなワークシーンがほとんどでしたが、近年続く記録的な猛暑により一般層への認知が飛躍的に拡大。ワークウェアメーカーだけでなく、アウトドアブランドやデザイナーズブランドなどが参入したことで、デザイン性が高いアイテムが数多く登場しています。 今年6月に開催された2027年春夏のパリメンズファッションウィークで大きな話題となったのが、「リック・オウエンス（Rick Owens）」が発表した空調ウェアです。協業した「アディダス（adidas）」が独自開発した空調システムを搭載しているそう。リック・オウエンスの前衛的なシルエットと、アディダスのテクノロジーが融合したインパクト満点の一着ですが、多くのミュージシャンに愛される両ブランドのコラボだけに、来年のフジロックのステージではこのアヴァンギャルドな空調ウェアを着用してパフォーマンスを行うミュージシャンが見られるかもしれませんね。 今後、さらに日常に溶け込んでいくことが予想される空調ウェア。野外フェスでも活躍しそうな、おしゃれな空調ウェアの注目4ブランドの新作をピックアップしました。 このコンテンツは FASHIONSNAP が配信しています。
item.title: 「カトパコ」着用で注目 野外フェスに着ていきたい最新デザイン空調ウェア4選

item.text: ​Constructed Views: Coyote Urbanism Presenting a critical urban analysis of Los Angeles through the lived experience of a coyote, Sukanya Mukherjee and Arnar Skarphedinsson explore the commodification of architecture in the city for the animated short Coyote Urbanism . Working between Los Angeles and Reykjavik, the Icelandic-Indian designer-director duo observe the impact of neoliberalization as the recognizable landscapes of Los Angeles give way to unfamiliar machines. When a solitary coyote's home becomes the next frontier of development, he wanders the streets and highways for a new place to rest, encountering the disparity and spectacle that shapes the urban environment, competing for dominance and leaving him little room to exist. Illustrating the urbanism of Los Angeles as a city formless in nature, instead tied together through individual experience and identity, Coyote Urbanism reframes the storied history of a resident population obscured in the city’s pursuit of “progress” – as the coyote is pushed to its edges by the infrastructure of machines taking over the urban fabric.
item.title: ​Constructed Views: Coyote Urbanism

analysis.chineseSummary: 德国设计工作室 Matter Of 通过《New New Typography》重新追问：今天的字体设计是否仍被 20 世纪现代主义规则限制。书中集合近百位创作者，以六套无衬线字体、243 个图例和六组理论提案讨论字体的可变性、身体性与政治关系。视觉上，它用黑白页面作为理性基础，再加入强烈高亮色、手写式标记和不断变化的字体组合，让整本书看起来像一本被持续修改的教材。对日报、杂志和品牌视觉而言，这种方法说明系统并不等于整齐一致，也可以通过受控的不稳定、标注感和版本变化制造阅读节奏。

item.text: ​In Motion: Doug Aitken’s Lightscape - Factory Tracing the search for individuality in a world of continuous rapid transformation, American artist Doug Aitken reveals a landscape of diversity on a cinematic journey through the U.S. for Lightscape – a new film-based installation on view at The Shed in New York City. Taken from the hour-long feature, Doug Aitken’s Lightscape - Factory observes factory workers against their automated, industrialized environment, entering into a rhythmic, kinetic synergy with the machines around them – featuring L.A. Dance Project’s Daphne Fernberger and Krump dancer Kevin “Konkrete” Davis, alongside actor-dancer Nathan Mitchell. A vulnerable, humanistic portrait that considers the hyper-connectivity of modern life, the film captures the blurring of humanity, technology and the environment, where bodies become a direct reflection of their mechanized surroundings through driving, twitching choreography. Led by a unique musical collaboration with the LA Master Chorale and the LA Phil, alongside sound design by Dražen Bošnjak, the visual and sonic landscapes shift gears as each character’s narrative evolves, guiding a state of transformation through haunting vocals and hardcore techno. Doug Aitken’s Lightscape is on view at The Shed in New York City until 13 September.
item.title: ​In Motion: Doug Aitken’s Lightscape - Factory

analysis.chineseSummary: 米兰摄影师 Cheyenne Clementi 与 Valentina Morandi 的新书《Never Alone》记录了 1997 至 2004 年穿行欧洲的自由派对与旅行者社群。照片中的卡车、废弃建筑、临时舞池和人物肖像，不只是锐舞现场的怀旧档案，也呈现了一种依靠移动、共享和自我组织形成的生活方式。对摄影团队而言，这组作品值得关注的不是复古滤镜，而是摄影师如何长期进入社群，用亲密距离记录服装、身体、空间和群体关系，让亚文化影像同时具有现场感与历史厚度。

analysis.selectionRationale: 这类题材提醒我们，真正有力量的青年文化摄影需要时间、信任和持续跟拍，而不是临时搭建几个亚文化符号。

analysis.chineseSummary: Aputure 解释 STORM X 系列为何采用约57至58度的原生泛光角：过宽光束虽然看似覆盖更大，却会把能量浪费在附件和场景之外；适度收窄能够提升反光罩与投射附件的效率，同时在经过柔光材料后保持足够均匀的面光。设计目标不是制造生硬聚光，而是在输出、附件兼容和人像肤质之间取得平衡，让同一灯具既能投射，也能转化为稳定柔和的主光。
analysis.chineseTitle: 为什么 STORM 主动缩窄光束：亮度、柔光附件与人像肤质之间的取舍
analysis.selectionRationale: 帮助摄影团队理解灯具参数背后的光学逻辑，避免只按瓦数和宣传亮度选择设备。

analysis.chineseSummary: 泰特现代美术馆带来 Ana Mendieta 十余年来在英国最深入的展览，重新梳理这位古巴裔艺术家以身体、土地、痕迹和仪式为核心的创作。她常把身体直接放入泥土、岩石、火焰或植物环境中，也会让人物退场，只留下轮廓与短暂痕迹，再通过摄影和影像保存现场。作品因此同时涉及女性身体、身份、流亡和人与自然的关系。对当代摄影与造型创作来说，启发在于如何减少服装与布景的堆积，转而用身体姿态、自然材质和消失感建立更强的象征性。
analysis.chineseTitle: 从身体到土地：重新理解 Ana Mendieta 的五个入口
analysis.selectionRationale: 她的作品可以帮助团队重新理解身体与场景的关系：环境不是陪衬，而是与人物共同完成画面的主体。

analysis.selectionRationale: 适合作为时尚人像团队的布光原则：从情绪和场景出发，而不是从设备清单出发。

analysis.chineseTitle: Margaret Howell 以最小改动重塑 Dr. Martens 1461
analysis.selectionRationale: 它适合参考如何把一个熟悉单品拍出新意：减少装饰，转而突出触感、制作细节和穿着后的时间变化。
